prevalence.source: Gastroenterology epidemiology syntheses; Brazilian IBD registries
prevalence.trend: increasing with urbanization and improved diagnosis

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to chronic disability and complications

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms plus endoscopy with biopsy; imaging (MR enterography/CT) for extent; exclude infection

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of thousands to low hundreds of thousands
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with access to biologics and specialist care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Colonoscopy and imaging available in tertiary centers; delays in some regions
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Corticosteroids for flares, immunomodulators (azathioprine), biologics (anti-TNF) via SUS pathways; surgery for complications
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to biologics and specialist IBD care outside major centers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing IBD centers and availability of essential immunosuppressants
